title: Meta warns iPhone users of Italian spyware campaign disguised as WhatsApp: Here's what you need to know
snippet: WhatsApp has alerted around 200 users in Italy about a malicious version of the app containing spyware developed by SIO. Victims were tricked into downloading the fake app through social engineering techniques.

snippet: Considering the MacBook Neo? Explore alternatives offering stronger performance, improved displays, and greater flexibility, helping you find a laptop that truly matches your needs and budget.

title: Android Auto left behind as OpenAI brings ChatGPT to Apple CarPlay: here's how it works
snippet: ChatGPT is now available on Apple CarPlay. The popular AI app can be used to interact with the AI via voice commands directly from their vehicle's dashboard.&nbsp;

title: iPhone 18 Pro, iPhone 18 Pro Max launching soon: Expected price, camera, processor and more
snippet: Apple could launch its iPhone 18 Pro series in September. The company could keep the same 6.3 and 6.9-inch displays on the two devices while also making many other smaller changes to the devices.&nbsp;

title: Perplexity AI accused of embedding ‘undetectable’ trackers for secretly routing sensitive user data to Meta and Google
snippet: Perplexity AI is facing a proposed class-action lawsuit for allegedly sharing sensitive user data with Meta and Google. The complaint claims that tracking software was embedded in the search engine to transmit personal information of users even in 'Incognito' mode.

title: Google ends 20-year restriction, finally allows users to change embarrassing Gmail usernames

title: Siri set for a big upgrade as Apple begins testing ChatGPT and Gemini like multi-request processing feature
snippet: Apple is testing a new Siri upgrade that lets users combine multiple requests in a single query. The feature is expected to debut at WWDC 2026 in June and roll out with iOS 27 in September.&nbsp;

title: X down again? Elon Musk's social media platform suffers another outage for thousands of users

snippet: A new AI machine called Fastsort-Textile is revolutionising textile recycling in China. title: Fintechs go easy on IPO plans as war rattles stock markets
snippet: Public listing plans for new-age fintech companies have received a major blow as the crisis in West Asia has battered the Indian rupee and stock valuations in Mumbai, prompting the highest ever annual withdrawal by overseas funds from locally listed equities.

title: Oracle layoffs: AI pivot triggers 10,000 job cuts in India
